restart_required.title: Restart required — TellStick Local v{new_version} installed
restart_required.description:
The TellStick Local app installed integration **v{new_version}** (currently loaded: v{current_version}).

Restart Home Assistant to activate the new version.

init.title: Restart Home Assistant
init.description:
Home Assistant will restart now to activate TellStick Local **v{new_version}**.

Click **Submit** to restart.

dev_channel.title: ⚠️ TellStick Local — you are on the dev channel
dev_channel.description:
You are using the **development channel** (`addon-tellsticklive-roosfork`). This channel receives the latest unreleased features and may occasionally be unstable.

**To switch to the stable channel:**
1. Go to **Settings → Apps → ⋮ → Repositories**
2. Remove `https://github.com/R00S/addon-tellsticklive-roosfork`
3. Add `https://github.com/R00S/addon-tellstick-local` (category: **App**)
4. Find **TellStick Local** in the app store and install it

Your existing configuration is preserved — same slug, same data directory.

hassio_confirm.title: TellStick Local detected
hassio_confirm.description:
The **{addon}** app is running and ready.

It will connect on **{host}** (command port **{command_port}**, event port **{event_port}**).

Click **Submit** to set up TellStick Local.

[📖 User Guide: Installation](https://github.com/R00S/addon-tellstick-local/blob/main/docs/USER_GUIDE.md#4-installation)

menu_options.duo: TellStick Duo (USB — connected to HAOS via the TellStick Local app)
menu_options.net: TellStick Net / ZNet (connected via Wi-Fi or Ethernet)

duo.title: Connect to TellStick daemon
duo.description:
Enter the host and ports of the TellStick Local app.

Find the correct hostname in the **app log** — look for the line `use host: …`. For apps installed from a custom repository the hostname includes a prefix (e.g. `e9305338-tellsticklive`).

data_description.host: Hostname of the TellStick Local app. Check the app log for the `use host:` line to find the correct value.

reconfigure.title: Update connection settings
reconfigure.description:
Update the hostname (or IP address) and ports for this TellStick entry.

This is useful when switching between the **stable** and **dev** channels — each channel has a different add-on hostname (e.g. `e9305338-tellsticklive` vs `abc12345-tellstick-local`).

Your stored devices and settings are preserved.

data_description.host: Hostname of the TellStick add-on. Check the app log for the `use host:` line to find the correct value.

net_discovery.title: Discover TellStick Net / ZNet
net_discovery.description:
Searching for TellStick Net devices on your local network…

If a device was found, select it from the dropdown. Otherwise enter the IP address of your TellStick Net / ZNet manually.

data_description.discovered_ip: Select a discovered device or choose manual entry.
data_description.host: Enter the IP address of your TellStick Net / ZNet device.

net_confirm.title: Confirm TellStick Net device
net_confirm.description:
Confirm connection to TellStick Net / ZNet at **{host}**.

The MAC address (if known): {mac}

data_description.host: IP address of the TellStick Net / ZNet device.

mirror_setup.title: Mirror / range extender
mirror_setup.description:
Would you like this TellStick to act as a **mirror / range extender** for an existing TellStick?

A mirror replicates all commands sent to the primary TellStick's devices, extending RF coverage. It also forwards received RF signals back to the primary for device detection.

The mirror does not have its own devices — it shares the primary's.

add_rf_device.title: New 433 MHz device detected

data_description.replace_device: Add as new device, add to an existing device (group), or replace an existing sensor (battery change).
data_description.ignore: Check to permanently hide this device. You can un-ignore it later from integration options.

error.cannot_connect: Cannot connect to the TellStick daemon. Check that the app is running and the host/ports are correct.
error.unknown: Unexpected error. See logs for details.

abort.already_configured: This TellStick daemon is already configured.
abort.device_added: Device added successfully! It will appear in your device list.
abort.device_replaced: Device replaced successfully! The existing entity now uses the new device ID. History and entity ID are preserved.
abort.device_ignored: Device ignored. It will no longer appear as discovered. You can un-ignore it from the integration options.
abort.entry_not_found: TellStick Local integration not found. Please set it up first.
abort.reconfigure_successful: Connection settings updated. The integration will reconnect using the new hostname.

data.automatic_add: Automatically add new devices (when off, new devices appear as 'Discovered' for manual confirmation)
data.detect_sartano: Detect sartano/codeswitch devices (off by default — arctech signals are often falsely decoded as sartano)
data.rtl433_sensors: Listen for rtl_433 sensors via MQTT (requires MQTT integration and rtl_433 add-on)

data_description.rtl433_sensors:
When enabled, sensor entities are auto-created for any 433 MHz sensor the rtl_433 add-on decodes. Requires the MQTT integration and the rtl_433 add-on configured with MQTT output.

[📖 User Guide: Pairing devices](https://github.com/R00S/addon-tellstick-local/blob/main/docs/USER_GUIDE.md#5-pairing-devices)

manage_device.title: Select a device
manage_device.description:
Pick a device to edit or delete.

To send a learn signal, go to the device page and press the **Send learn signal** button.

device_detail.title: Edit device
device_detail.description:
**{device_uid}**

Protocol: **{protocol}** / Model: **{model}**

Change the name or parameters below. Changing house/unit codes or sensor ID will update the device identity — entity ID and history are preserved.

data_description.sensor_id: The numeric ID from the RF signal. Change this when a sensor gets a new ID (e.g. after battery replacement) to keep entity history.

teach_device.title: Send learn signal
teach_device.description:
This will send a learn/pairing signal for **{name}** ({protocol}/{model}).

Put your 433 MHz receiver in **learn mode** now, then click **Submit**.

move_device.title: Move to another instance
move_device.description:
Move **{name}** to a different TellStick instance.

The entity keeps its existing entity ID and state history.

move_device_confirm.title: Confirm move
move_device_confirm.description:
Move **{name}** from **{src}** to **{dst}**?

The entity ID and state history are preserved. The integration will reload to apply the change.

group_device.title: Group under a shared device
group_device.description:
Group **{name}** with other devices under a shared Home Assistant device.

All devices with the same group name appear as entities under one device in Devices & Services.

Existing groups: **{existing_groups}**

Leave blank to remove from group (standalone device).

[📖 User Guide: Grouping devices](https://github.com/R00S/addon-tellstick-local/blob/main/docs/USER_GUIDE.md#72-grouping-any-device-under-a-shared-ha-device)

data_description.group_uid: Enter a group name (e.g. "Living Room"). All devices with the same name share one HA device. Leave blank to ungroup.

data.ignore: Also ignore this device (prevents automatic re-detection)

remove_devices.title: Remove multiple devices
remove_devices.description: Select devices to remove from this integration.

data.ignore: Also ignore removed devices (prevents automatic re-detection)

manage_ignored.title: Manage ignored devices
manage_ignored.description: Select devices to un-ignore. Un-ignored devices can be detected again when they send an RF signal.

abort.teach_sent: Learn signal sent successfully! If the receiver was in learn mode, it should now be paired.
abort.device_moved: Device moved successfully. Both TellStick instances will reload to apply the change.
abort.mirror_no_settings: This TellStick is configured as a mirror — it inherits all settings from the primary entry. To change settings, open the primary TellStick entry instead.

menu_options.by_protocol_native: Add by protocol (TellStick native — unit-1 fix)
menu_options.by_protocol_native_nofix: Add by protocol (TellStick native — no unit fix)
menu_options.generic_rf: Add Generic RF device (record from remote)

by_brand.title: Add by brand
by_brand.description: Pick your device type and give it a name.

by_protocol.title: Add by protocol
by_protocol.description: Pick the protocol and model, then give it a name.

by_protocol_raw.title: Add by protocol (raw pulse)
by_protocol_raw.description:
These protocols use **raw pulse-train encoders** that bypass ZNet firmware bugs. Not yet tested on real hardware.

Pick the protocol and model, then give it a name.

by_protocol_native.title: Add by protocol (TellStick native — unit-1 fix)
by_protocol_native.description:
These protocols are handled by the TellStick firmware's built-in protocol stack. The firmware's **unit+1 bug** is compensated for (we subtract 1), but only **house** and **unit** parameters are passed — protocols needing code, system, or fade may not work correctly.

Pick the protocol and model, then give it a name.

by_protocol_native_nofix.title: Add by protocol (TellStick native — no unit fix)
by_protocol_native_nofix.description:
Same as native encoding but does **NOT** compensate for the firmware's unit+1 bug — the unit value is sent as-is. Use this to test whether the bug actually applies to your device.

Pick the protocol and model, then give it a name.

params.title: Device parameters
params.description: Enter the parameters for **{device_type}**. Ranges and defaults match the hardware.

confirm.title: Send pairing signal
confirm.description:
**{name}** ({protocol}/{model}) will learn house code **{house}**, unit **{unit}**.

Put your 433 MHz receiver in **learn mode** now, then click **Submit** — the pairing signal is sent immediately.

[📖 User Guide: Self-learning teach](https://github.com/R00S/addon-tellstick-local/blob/main/docs/USER_GUIDE.md#52-self-learning-teach-add-device-button)

generic_rf.title: Generic RF device — record from remote
generic_rf.description:
Record a 433 MHz signal from any remote and replay it via the TellStick Duo. This only works on TellStick Duo (not Net/ZNet).

Enter a name for this device.

data.name: Device name
data.repeat_count: Repeat count (how many times to transmit)

data_description.repeat_count: More repeats improve reliability. 10 is a good default.

generic_rf_listen_on.title: Record ON signal

generic_rf_confirm_on.title: ON signal captured
generic_rf_confirm_on.description:
Signal captured from **{model}** ({timings_count} timings).

Do you want to record a separate OFF signal? If not, the ON signal will be used for both ON and OFF.

generic_rf_listen_off.title: Record OFF signal

generic_rf_confirm_on_light.title: ON signal captured
generic_rf_confirm_on_light.description:
Signal captured from **{model}** ({timings_count} timings).

Do you want to record a separate OFF signal and/or dim levels? If not, the ON signal will be used for all states.

generic_rf_dim_choice.title: Dim levels
generic_rf_dim_choice.description:
Do you want to record specific dim level signals for **{name}**?

This lets you dim the device to preset levels.

generic_rf_dim_level_select.title: Select dim level
generic_rf_dim_level_select.description:
Which dim level do you want to record next for **{name}**?

Already recorded: {recorded}

generic_rf_listen_dim.title: Record {dim_level}% dim signal

generic_rf_listen_up.title: Record UP signal

generic_rf_confirm_up.title: UP signal captured
generic_rf_confirm_up.description:
UP signal captured from **{model}** ({timings_count} timings).

Now record the DOWN signal.

generic_rf_listen_down.title: Record DOWN signal

generic_rf_confirm_down.title: DOWN signal captured
generic_rf_confirm_down.description:
DOWN signal captured from **{model}** ({timings_count} timings).

Optionally record a STOP signal.

generic_rf_listen_stop.title: Record STOP signal

error.invalid_code: Parameter values must not be empty.
error.teach_failed: Failed to send pairing signal. Check that the TellStick app is running.
error.no_signal: No signal captured — make sure rtl_433 add-on is running and press the remote button again.

abort.device_added: Device paired successfully! It will appear in your device list shortly.
abort.mirror_is_secondary: This TellStick is a mirror — devices must be added through the primary hub entry.
